205	## Mathlib dependency anchors (`\mathlibok`)
206	
207	When your chapter's proofs rely on a definition or lemma that **Mathlib already provides** (and the project will not re-prove), it is good practice — not just a bare `\uses{}` to nowhere — to make that dependency a first-class blueprint block: a **Mathlib dependency anchor**. Doing so:

210	- gives `leandag` a real node, so the `\uses{}` resolves (no broken/unknown ref) and the node's effort is **done** rather than ∞;

217	  \label{lem:hensel_mathlib}

219	  \mathlibok

8040	  \(V\), is identified with the absolute cohomology presheaf

8043	  the second argument \(Y\) is identified with the \(n\)-th cohomology of the \(\operatorname{Hom}\)

8045	  of the Ext-to-cohomology-class equivalence and the cohomology-class-to-homology equivalence of the

8048	  \mathcal{I}^k)\) is identified with \(\Gamma(f^{-1}(V), \mathcal{I}^k) = (f_*\mathcal{I}^k)(V)\) by

8085	  \(\Gamma(W, j_* M) = \Gamma(j^{-1}(W), M)\) --- the pushforward along \(j\), forgotten to
8086	  abelian groups, viewed as a presheaf and evaluated at \(W\). It is an additive functor, and
8087	  its \(q\)-th right derived functor applied to \(H\) computes the absolute cohomology
8088	  \(H^q(j^{-1}(W), H)\).

8093	  forgetful functor to presheaves of abelian groups (additive), and evaluation at \(W\)
8094	  (additive). A composite of additive functors is additive; the additivity follows from the additivity of
8095	  each factor. The identification of its right derived functor with
8096	  absolute cohomology is the presheaf description of
8097	  Lemma~\ref{lem:higher_direct_image_presheaf}.

input.new_string:
\emph{(2) Serre vanishing for a general affine open.} The Serre vanishing of
  Lemma~\ref{lem:affine_serre_vanishing} is pinned to the \emph{top} open \(\top\) of a spectrum
  \(\operatorname{Spec} R\): it kills \(\operatorname{Ext}^q(j_!\mathcal{O}_\top, -)\) for \(q \geq 1\).
  The cohomology that actually occurs here is over a \emph{general} affine open --- the preimage
  \(j^{-1}(V)\) (respectively \(U \cap f^{-1}(V)\)) --- so the pinned vanishing must be transported.
  We do this in three named sub-steps, \emph{without} building a fresh affine-cover system on the
  general affine open.

  \emph{(2a) The general affine open is an affine scheme.} The immersion \(j\) is an affine morphism:
  for \(U\) affine and \(X\) separated, \(j : U \hookrightarrow X\) is affine, so the preimage of every
  affine open of \(X\) is an affine open of \(U\). In particular \(j^{-1}(V) = U \cap V\) (and likewise
  \(U \cap f^{-1}(V)\)) is an affine open of the affine scheme \(U\), hence an affine scheme in its own
  right.

  \emph{(2b) Reduction to the right-derived sections functor.} By the presheaf description
  (Lemma~\ref{lem:higher_direct_image_presheaf}), evaluating the pushed-forward resolution complex at
  \(V\) and taking homology computes \(H^q(j^{-1}(V), \mathcal{H})\) as the \(q\)-th right derived
  functor of the sections functor \(\Gamma(j^{-1}(V), -)\) applied to \(\mathcal{H}\). By the
  corepresentability isomorphism of Lemma~\ref{lem:sectionsFunctorCorepIso},
  \(\Gamma(j^{-1}(V), -) \cong \operatorname{Hom}(j_!\mathcal{O}_{j^{-1}(V)}, -)\) as additive functors,
  and Lemma~\ref{lem:rightDerivedNatIso} transports this isomorphism to their right derived functors;
  thus \(H^q(j^{-1}(V), \mathcal{H}) \cong \operatorname{Ext}^q(j_!\mathcal{O}_{j^{-1}(V)}, \mathcal{H})\).

  \emph{(2c) Transport along the spectrum isomorphism.} By (2a) the affine open \(j^{-1}(V)\) carries
  the canonical scheme isomorphism \(j^{-1}(V) \cong \operatorname{Spec}\Gamma(j^{-1}(V), \mathcal{O})\)
  of Lemma~\ref{lem:isoSpec_mathlib}. Under the module-category equivalence this iso induces, the
  sections functor \(\Gamma(j^{-1}(V), -)\) corresponds to the global-sections functor
  \(\Gamma(\top, -)\) on the spectrum and \(j_!\mathcal{O}_{j^{-1}(V)}\) to \(j_!\mathcal{O}_\top\);
  transporting the right-derived vanishing across this identification (again via
  Lemma~\ref{lem:rightDerivedNatIso}) carries \(\operatorname{Ext}^q(j_!\mathcal{O}_{j^{-1}(V)},
  \mathcal{H})\) to \(\operatorname{Ext}^q(j_!\mathcal{O}_\top, \mathcal{H}')\) on
  \(\operatorname{Spec}\Gamma(j^{-1}(V), \mathcal{O})\), which is the pinned \(\top\)-case
  \(\operatorname{affine\_serre\_vanishing}\) and vanishes for \(q \geq 1\). This whole transport is
  packaged as Lemma~\ref{lem:sectionsFunctor_isoSpec_transport}.
